paragraph: Single-family homes in Ramsey are moving in a real seller's market. Over the last six months, 52 homes sold at a median of $933,000, with over half going above asking and a sale-to-list ratio sitting at 1.01. Median days on market landed at 18, which tells you that priced-right homes are attracting multiple buyers and sellers don't have much incentive to negotiate hard. The range is wide, from $470,000 to $2,450,000, so price point matters less than condition and location in determining how fast a home moves. Condos and townhouses paint a different picture. Twenty-eight sales at a median of $590,000 show stronger buyer activity here, with 75 percent selling above asking and a median days on market of 12. The sale-to-list ratio of 1.06 means these homes are outperforming their list prices by a larger margin than single-family properties are. If you're shopping in Ramsey, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home, or the lower price point and faster pace of a condo or townhouse.
